schools.summary: Gill: Moses is called 'the man of God,' raised up of God and eminently qualified by him with gifts for the work; this blessing given before his death.

themes: this is the blessing, wherewith Moses the man of God blessed the children of Israel before his death
the dying blessing of a faithful leader
the legacy of the great mediator (Gen. 49:28)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses was in some things an eye and ear witness, and in others inspired by the Spirit; the LORD came from Sinai with ten thousands of saints and a fiery law from his right hand.

themes: The LORD came from Sinai... he came with ten thousands of saints: from his right hand went a fiery law
the glorious majesty of God's appearing
the angels at the giving of the law (Heb. 12:22)

schools.summary: Gill: his giving the law in such a glorious manner was an instance of his love, a distinguishing blessing; how much more the love of God to his spiritual Israel; all his saints in his hand.

themes: he loved the people; all his saints are in thy hand: and they sat down at thy feet
God's people held safe in his hand
none shall pluck them out of my hand (John 10:28)

schools.summary: Gill: the law came from God, a declaration of his will, delivered to Moses and by him to the children of Israel, the inheritance of the congregation.

themes: Moses commanded us a law, even the inheritance of the congregation of Jacob
the law a precious heritage, not a burden
taken as an heritage for ever (Ps. 119:111)

schools.summary: Gill: this is God, as Jarchi — the government of Israel was a theocracy, their laws immediately from God, guided and directed by him.

themes: And he was king in Jeshurun, when the heads of the people... were gathered together
God himself the King of his people
the LORD is our king; he will save us (Isa. 33:22)

schools.summary: Gill: Reuben should continue as a tribe and not be extinct, though not excel because of the sin of their progenitor; with special regard to their preservation beyond Jordan.

themes: Let Reuben live, and not die; and let not his men be few
preservation rather than pre-eminence
in wrath remember mercy (Hab. 3:2)

schools.summary: Gill: the blessing of Judah, the supplement made in the Targums; his hands should be sufficient for him, and the LORD a help against his enemies.

themes: Hear, LORD, the voice of Judah... let his hands be sufficient for him; and be thou an help to him
prayer heard and help in war
the LORD is my helper (Ps. 118:6-7)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses said of the tribe of Levi.
Keil & Delitzsch: the Thummim and Urim with the holy one, proved at Massah and Meribah.

themes: Let thy Thummim and thy Urim be with thy holy one
the priestly oracle and its sacred trust
a shadow of Christ the holy High Priest (Heb. 7:26)

schools.summary: Gill: some understand it of the high priests not defiling themselves for father or mother; others of having no respect to them in judgment, but determining justly; they kept God's covenant.

themes: Who said unto his father and to his mother, I have not seen him... for they have observed thy word
faithfulness setting God's cause above kin
love to God above father and mother (Matt. 10:37)

schools.summary: Gill: the priests and Levites, dispersed among the tribes, taught the people the laws, statutes, and ordinances of the Lord.
Keil & Delitzsch: the teaching and the offering.

themes: They shall teach Jacob thy judgments... they shall put incense before thee, and whole burnt sacrifice
the double charge of word and worship
the priest's lips should keep knowledge (Mal. 2:7)

schools.summary: Gill: Levi's substance lay in tithes, firstfruits, and the cities and devoted things given them; God would accept their work and smite those who rose against them.

themes: Bless, LORD, his substance, and accept the work of his hands: smite through the loins of them that rise against him
the ministry maintained and defended
touch not mine anointed (Ps. 105:15)

schools.summary: Gill: Benjamin named next to Levi because the temple, where the priests officiated, was in or near his tribe; the beloved should dwell in safety and be covered by the LORD.

themes: The beloved of the LORD shall dwell in safety by him... and he shall dwell between his shoulders
the security and tender nearness of God's beloved
he shall carry them in his bosom (Isa. 40:11)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses said of the tribe of Joseph.
Keil & Delitzsch: the land blessed for the precious things of heaven, the dew, and the deep beneath.

themes: Blessed of the LORD be his land, for the precious things of heaven, for the dew, and for the deep
the richest of temporal blessings
every good gift from the Father (James 1:17)

schools.summary: Gill: the sun has a wonderful influence on the fruits of the earth, to produce, ripen, and perfect them; Jarchi says the land of Joseph lay open to the sun; the moon put forth its precious things.

themes: the precious fruits brought forth by the sun, and... the precious things put forth by the moon
the heavenly lights God's instruments of fruitfulness
he appointed the moon for seasons (Ps. 104:19)

schools.summary: Gill: the ancient mountains, from the beginning of the world, for which the land of Joseph was famous, as Gilead, Bashan, and Mount Ephraim.

themes: the chief things of the ancient mountains, and for the precious things of the lasting hills
God's gifts abundant and enduring
the blessing unto the everlasting hills (Gen. 49:26)

schools.summary: Gill: the corn, grass, and cattle of the earth, for which Bashan was famous; the good will of him that dwelt in the bush upon Joseph.

themes: for the good will of him that dwelt in the bush: let the blessing come upon the head of Joseph
the favour of God himself the deepest blessing
in his favour is life (Ps. 30:5)

schools.summary: Gill: the firstling of his bullock, comely and majestic; with his horns he should push the people to the ends of the earth — the ten thousands of Ephraim and thousands of Manasseh.

themes: His glory is like the firstling of his bullock, and his horns are like the horns of unicorns
Joseph's strength and victory over the nations
in thy name will we tread them under (Ps. 44:5)

schools.summary: Gill: Zebulun and Issachar joined, brethren of the same mother; Zebulun the younger set before Issachar, as in Jacob's blessing.
Keil & Delitzsch: the rejoicing of Zebulun abroad and Issachar at home.

themes: Rejoice, Zebulun, in thy going out; and, Issachar, in thy tents
God's blessing on every honest calling
blessed coming in and going out (Deut. 28:6)

schools.summary: Gill, with the Targums: the calling of the people to the mountain of the sanctuary, the temple Moses foresaw; they should suck the abundance of the seas and treasures hid in the sand.

themes: They shall call the people unto the mountain... for they shall suck of the abundance of the seas
worship joined to wealth, gain sanctified
the abundance of the sea converted to thee (Isa. 60:5)

schools.summary: Gill: he dwelt as a lion, tearing the arm and the crown of the head.
Clarke: deliverance out of distress is called enlarging (Ps. 4:1), referring to God's delivering Gad.

themes: Blessed be he that enlargeth Gad: he dwelleth as a lion
God's enlarging grace and Gad's valour
thou hast enlarged me when in distress (Ps. 4:1)

schools.summary: Gill, with Jarchi: the first part was the portion of Sihon and Og, the firstfruits of the land subdued, which Gad chose.
Keil & Delitzsch: he came with the heads of the people and executed the justice of the LORD.

themes: he provided the first part for himself... and he came with the heads of the people, he executed the justice of the LORD
self-interest rightly ordered to duty
look on the things of others (Phil. 2:4)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses said of the tribe of Dan.
Henry: where Jacob compared Dan to a serpent for subtlety, Moses compares him to a lion for courage and resolution.

themes: Dan is a lion's whelp: he shall leap from Bashan
Dan's courage and resolution
the righteous are bold as a lion (Prov. 28:1)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses said of the tribe of Naphtali.
Keil & Delitzsch: Naphtali satisfied with favour, full of the LORD's blessing, possessing the west and the south.

themes: O Naphtali, satisfied with favour, and full with the blessing of the LORD
the favour of God the true satisfaction
satisfied with the goodness of thy house (Ps. 65:4)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses said of the tribe of Asher.
Keil & Delitzsch: Asher blessed with children, acceptable to his brethren, dipping his foot in oil.

themes: Let Asher be blessed with children... and let him dip his foot in oil
increase, favour, and abundance
my cup runneth over (Ps. 23:5)

schools.summary: Gill: the shoes of iron and brass denote a sure footing and defence; the strength promised to equal the days.

themes: Thy shoes shall be iron and brass; and as thy days, so shall thy strength be
strength measured to each day's need
my grace is sufficient for thee (2 Cor. 12:9)

schools.summary: Gill: Jeshurun is Israel; the Lord was their God in a special sense, and there is none like him for the perfection of his being; he rides upon the heaven in their help.

themes: There is none like unto the God of Jeshurun, who rideth upon the heaven in thy help
the incomparable LORD, an almighty helper
who is like unto thee, O LORD? (Exod. 15:11)

schools.summary: Gill: God is eternal, the Ancient of days; the same true of Christ, the everlasting Father, the true God and eternal life; the everlasting arms underneath, and he thrusts out the enemy.

themes: The eternal God is thy refuge, and underneath are the everlasting arms
an almighty, unfailing strength upholding his people
the LORD my rock... my high tower (Ps. 18:2)

schools.summary: Gill: Israel, the Canaanites thrust out, should dwell in safety or confidently, without fear, though alone, a separate and distinct people; the land yielded corn and wine.

themes: Israel then shall dwell in safety alone: the fountain of Jacob shall be upon a land of corn and wine
a separated people kept secure under God
the people shall dwell alone (Num. 23:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the conclusion of the blessing — Israel happy in having God for their God and so many good things promised; a people saved by the LORD, his shield and sword.

themes: Happy art thou, O Israel: who is like unto thee, O people saved by the LORD
the blessedness of having the LORD for God and Saviour
happy is the people whose God is the LORD (Ps. 144:15)
